articles.headline: Kane eyes Lewandowski record: Tracking Europe's major goal, assist records
articles.description: Harry Kane is chasing Robert Lewandowski's incredible Bundesliga goal tally, while Kylian Mbappé seeks Champions League history. Here's the latest in Europe's record goal and assist tallies.

images.name: Harry Kane [1296x729]
images.caption: Kane scored his 20th Bundesliga goal of the season today after just 16 games.
images.credit: Photo by Tom Weller/picture alliance via Getty Images

images.name: Manchester United's Bruno Fernandes [1296x729]
images.caption: Bruno Fernandes' assist tally took a sizeable leap after Ruben Amorim was sacked as Manchester United manager.

images.name: Lamine Yamal, Kylian Mbappe [1296x729]
images.caption: Neither Kylian Mbappé nor Lamine Yamal are likely to break Lionel Messi's single-season goal and assist marks in LaLiga.

images.name: Inter Milan's Lautaro Martinez [1296x729]
images.caption: Lautaro Martinez easily leads the Seire A scoring chart, but he won't get near the single-season record.

images.name: Mason Greenwood aug 2025 [1296x729]
images.caption: Mason Greenwood was made to share the Ligue 1 top scorer title last season.

images.name: Vinicus Junior, Kylian Mbappe [1296x729]
images.caption: Kylian Mbappé is within reach of Cristiano Ronaldo's single-season Champions League goal record.
images.credit: David S. Bustamante/Real Madrid via Getty Images

images.name: Bayern Munich holds on for 3-2 win
images.caption: Harry Kane scores a brace as Bayern Munich beat Eintracht Frankfurt 3-2.

articles.headline: Transfer rumors, news: Man United keeping tabs on Stuttgart's Andrés
articles.description: Manchester United could look to the Bundesliga for their newest midfield signing. Transfer Talk has the latest.

images.name: Chema Andrés [1296x729]
images.credit: (Photo by Harry Langer/picture alliance via Getty Images)

images.name: Is Lamine Yamal the best player in the world?
images.caption: The 'ESPN FC' crew react to Lamine Yamal becoming the youngest player to score a LaLiga hat-trick this century as Barcelona beat Villarreal 4-1.

articles.headline: Arsenal win ugly vs. Chelsea; Bayern close in on Bundesliga title; more
articles.description: Arsenal muscled past Chelsea on Sunday, but coach Mikel Arteta can't be happy at how difficult things look for his team. Also: Bayern Munich likely wrapped up the Bundesliga with a Der Klassiker win.

images.name: Arsenal celeb v Chelsea 260302 [1296x729]
images.credit: Stuart MacFarlane/Arsenal FC via Getty Images

images.name: Can Arsenal ride set-pieces all the way to the title?
images.caption: Shaka Hislop and Steve Nicol wonder if Arsenal are too reliant on set-pieces to win the Premier League title.

keyEvents.text: Own Goal by Nordi Mukiele, Bayer 04 Leverkusen. Bayer 04 Leverkusen 0, VfL Wolfsburg 1.

Goal! Bayer 04 Leverkusen 1, VfL Wolfsburg 1. Florian Wirtz (Bayer 04 Leverkusen) right footed shot from the centre of the box to the bottom left corner. Assisted by Granit Xhaka following a set piece situation.

Goal! Bayer 04 Leverkusen 2, VfL Wolfsburg 1. Jonathan Tah (Bayer 04 Leverkusen) header from the centre of the box to the bottom right corner. Assisted by Álex Grimaldo with a cross following a corner.
Jeanuël Belocian (Bayer 04 Leverkusen) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Goal! Bayer 04 Leverkusen 2, VfL Wolfsburg 2. Sebastiaan Bornauw (VfL Wolfsburg) header from very close range to the centre of the goal. Assisted by Jakub Kaminski with a cross following a corner.
Substitution, VfL Wolfsburg. Konstantinos Koulierakis replaces Ridle Baku.
Goal! Bayer 04 Leverkusen 2, VfL Wolfsburg 3. Mattias Svanberg (VfL Wolfsburg) right footed shot from outside the box to the bottom right corner. Assisted by Konstantinos Koulierakis.
Álex Grimaldo (Bayer 04 Leverkusen) is shown the yellow card.
First Half ends, Bayer 04 Leverkusen 2, VfL Wolfsburg 3.
Substitution, Bayer 04 Leverkusen. Piero Hincapié replaces Jeanuël Belocian.
Substitution, Bayer 04 Leverkusen. Jeremie Frimpong replaces Nordi Mukiele.
Goal! Bayer 04 Leverkusen 3, VfL Wolfsburg 3. Piero Hincapié (Bayer 04 Leverkusen) header from very close range to the top left corner. Assisted by Aleix García with a cross following a corner.
Tiago Tomás (VfL Wolfsburg) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Amine Adli (Bayer 04 Leverkusen) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Substitution, Bayer 04 Leverkusen. Martin Terrier replaces Amine Adli.
Substitution, Bayer 04 Leverkusen. Victor Boniface replaces Patrik Schick.
Substitution, VfL Wolfsburg. Yannick Gerhardt replaces Tiago Tomás because of an injury.
Substitution, VfL Wolfsburg. Salih Özcan replaces Mattias Svanberg.
Substitution, VfL Wolfsburg. Aster Vranckx replaces Patrick Wimmer.
Substitution, Bayer 04 Leverkusen. Exequiel Palacios replaces Aleix García.
Cédric Zesiger (VfL Wolfsburg) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Jeremie Frimpong (Bayer 04 Leverkusen) is shown the yellow card.
Yannick Gerhardt (VfL Wolfsburg) is shown the red card for violent conduct.
Kamil Grabara (VfL Wolfsburg) is shown the yellow card.
Maximilian Arnold (VfL Wolfsburg) is shown the yellow card.
Substitution, VfL Wolfsburg. Denis Vavro replaces Mohammed Amoura.
Goal! Bayer 04 Leverkusen 4, VfL Wolfsburg 3. Victor Boniface (Bayer 04 Leverkusen) left footed shot from the centre of the box to the bottom left corner following a corner.
Jakub Kaminski (VfL Wolfsburg) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Second Half ends, Bayer 04 Leverkusen 4, VfL Wolfsburg 3.

commentary.text: Lineups are announced and players are warming up.
Delay in match because of an injury Mohammed Amoura (VfL Wolfsburg).
Delay over. They are ready to continue.
First Half begins.

tracking.trackingName: SOC_One-Play (Bayer Leverkusen vs. VfL Wolfsburg - Game Highlights) 2024/09/22 ESHEET

article.story:
Bayer Leverkusen pulled off a stunning Bundesliga comeback when they twice fought back from a goal down as Victor Boniface netted a 93rd-minute winner in a 4-3 home victory over VfL Wolfsburg on Sunday.

Second-placed Leverkusen have nine points from four games, level with SC Freiburg and Eintracht Frankfurt and two ahead of Borussia Dortmund and RB Leipzig, who play later on Sunday.

Despite taking home the three points, it was far from a convincing performance from the champions, with midfielder Granit Xhaka criticising his side's defending.

"This is a huge wake-up call for all of us. We can't continue with this performance," Xhaka told DAZN. "We can't defend so naively. That's not good enough and shouldn't happen at this level. A top team doesn't concede two goals in nine minutes! It was all far too easy."

Coach Xabi Alonso opted to shuffle his pack, making five changes to the team that beat Feyenoord 4-0 in the Champions League, and Leverkusen suffered a nightmare start as they conceded from a Nordi Mukiele own goal in the fourth minute.

Leverkusen levelled nine minutes later when Florian Wirtz found space on the edge of the box and swept his shot into the bottom corner, before an unmarked Jonathan Tah scored their second with a cool header at the back post.

The lead was short-lived as the visitors equalised from a Sebastiaan Bornauw header before Mattias Svanberg went on a driving run in Leverkusen's half and curled the ball past goalkeeper Lukas Hradecky on the stroke of halftime.

Leverkusen looked more assured after Alsonso made two changes during the break and their pressure quickly told, as substitute Piero Hincapié headed home a corner in the 49th minute.

Alonso brought on attackers Boniface and Martin Terrier with Leverkusen desperately seeking a winner and Wolfsburg quickly faded under the pressure.

The visitors suffered another blow when substitute Yannick Gerhardt was shown a red card in the 88th minute after clattering into Jeremie Frimpong from behind and then stepping on the defender's ankle.

Sensing disarray in Wolfsburg's ranks, the hosts surged forward and Nigerian Boniface, who started on the bench despite scoring twice in Leverkusen's last league game, pounced on a loose ball in the penalty area and rifled home the winner.

"We knew how Wolfsburg would play and what we were up against... we really wanted to turn the game around for our fans," Boniface said. "We managed to do that. Now, of course, we're very happy."

The win will fill Leverkusen with confidence ahead of their daunting clash against in-form league-leaders Bayern Munich on Saturday.
